content.problemStatement:
Given NNN students standing in a row, each student iii has an **distinct** achievement score AiA_iAiâ€‹ provided in an array A=[A1,A2,â€¦,An]A = [A_1, A_2, \dots, A_n]A=[A1â€‹,A2â€‹,â€¦,Anâ€‹]. The array AAA consists of distinct integers from 111 to NNN.

A student iii ( 1â‰¤iâ‰¤N1 \leq i \leq N1â‰¤iâ‰¤N ) will be happy if their achievement score AiA_iAiâ€‹ is greater than the achievement scores of all the students standing before them in the array.

The task is to print whether a student is happy or not.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN â€” the number of students.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,â€¦,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1â€‹,A2â€‹,â€¦,ANâ€‹, denoting the achievement scores of the students.




Output Format


For each test case, print a single line containing NNN integers. Print ithi^{th}ith integer as 111 if the ithi^{th}ith student is happy, otherwise print 000.




Each test case's output should be on a new line, with the integers separated by spaces.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 4Ă—1054 \times 10^54Ă—105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1:

There is no student behind student 111, so he is happy.
For student 222, there is student 111 with greater achievement score, so he is not happy.
Similarly for student 333, there is student 111 with greater score, so he is not happy.
Student 444 has greater achievement score than student 111, student 222, and student 333, so student 444 is happy.
Student 555 has greater score than all other students, so he is happy.
